text: The research examined how three heat treatment methods affect the Ti555211 titanium alloy performance and fracture characteristics. Beta-annealing treatment at 870-890°C for 8 hours retains high strength but shows almost no ductility. Tensile strength reached 1120-1315 MPa with brittle cleavage fracture mode. Double annealing treatment at 840-880°C prioritized ductility enhancement with elongation reaching 54% and tensile strength of 1082-1109 MPa. The fracture morphology showed ductile dimples indicating plastic deformation capacity. Solid-solution and aging treatment with optimal process at 820°C for 2 hours and aging at 560°C for 12 hours achieved maximum tensile strength of 1404 MPa with elongation of 11%. The solid-solution and aging approach provided superior comprehensive mechanical properties, demonstrating both high strength and a certain degree of ductility.

meta.title: Impact of Heat Treatment on the Mechanical Properties and Fracture Morphology of Ti555211 Alloy

text: The study investigated a novel low-cost titanium alloy Ti-3Al-5Mo-4Cr-2Zr-1Fe (Ti-35421). Researchers examined the influence of heat treatment on the microstructure characteristics and mechanical properties of this material. Solution treatment was performed at temperatures from 760°C to 840°C for 0.5-1 hour with air cooling. Aging treatment was applied at 500°C to 560°C for 2-16 hours. When solution-treated at 780°C for 1 hour followed by aging exceeding 8 hours, the alloy achieved tensile strength of 1172-1459 MPa, yield strength of 1135-1355 MPa, and elongation of 5-12%. The precipitation of secondary alpha phase is closely related to heat treatment parameters. Volume fraction and size of secondary alpha phase increased with higher solution temperatures or extended aging times. The alloy maintains mechanical properties and reduces the cost of materials compared with Ti-B19 alloy, making it viable for aerospace applications.

meta.title: The Effect of Heat Treatment on the Microstructure and Mechanical Properties of Ti-3Al-5Mo-4Cr-2Zr-1Fe Alloy

text: The research examines laser powder bed fusion processing of Ti-24Nb-4Zr-8Sn (Ti2448), a biocompatible titanium alloy. Solution treatment was applied at 750°C for 1 hour with different cooling methods including furnace cooling, air cooling, and slow water quenching. Aging treatment at 500°C for 4 hours following slow water quenching increased hardness to 290 HV5 and yield strength to 819 MPa with elongation of 12%. Furnace-cooled specimens achieved hardness of 305 HV5, yield strength of 805 MPa, and ultimate tensile strength of 931 MPa with elongation of 11%. As-built specimens showed hardness of 219 HV5, yield strength of 490 MPa, and tensile strength of 700 MPa with elongation of 22%. The controlled precipitation of alpha phases through tailored heat treatments enabled adjustment of mechanical performance. LPBF-processed Ti2448 achieves comparable mechanical properties to conventionally processed variants while maintaining acceptable ductility.

meta.title: Heat Treatments of Metastable Beta Titanium Alloy Ti-24Nb-4Zr-8Sn Processed by Laser Powder Bed Fusion
